elements.text: The Scarred Gorge is choked with red dust and the screams of dying traditional cultivators. Energy blasts from the expansionist sect's vanguard shear through the stone ridges, shattering the defensive lines.
Lyra stands near a collapsed trench, her sword gleaming with a fading light, trying to rally a group of panicked survivors.
Hold the line! Fall back to the secondary ridge in formation! Do not let them scatter you!
The retreating cultivators ignore her, fleeing past in a chaotic rout as another barrage of explosive qi rains down.
Master Valerius drives his heavy blade into the trembling earth to anchor himself, his breath heavy, his formidable frame bearing the physical toll of the onslaught.
It is a tide, child. You cannot command a broken dam to hold back the sea. Our techniques are too slow for this chaos.
Kaelen stands on a rocky outcrop just above them, his eyes darting across the gorge, analyzing the enemy's advance with cold, unblinking precision.
They are overextending their vanguard to secure a quick slaughter. They expect us to cluster on the ridge. It is a predictable tactical error.
A tactical error? They are butchering us! We have to stand and protect the wounded!
elements.expression: furious, pointing her blade at him

elements.text: Kaelen steps down from the ledge, completely ignoring her anger.
The wounded are already dead; they simply haven't stopped moving. If we collapse the eastern archway now, we bury the vanguard—along with the thirty traditionalists bottlenecked beneath it.
You would crush our own people? To buy yourself a minute? You are a monster, Kaelen!

To buy us the gorge. The collapse funnels the remaining sect forces into the creek bed where your archers actually have a line of sight. It yields a sixty percent survival rate for active combatants. Your way yields zero.
Lyra turns to Valerius, searching for a reprimand, but the old master looks down at his blood-stained hands, then toward the crumbling ridge.
He is correct, Lyra. The traditional formations are dust. If we do not cut the limb, the poison takes the heart.
Master Valerius, your qi is the only force strong enough to trigger the collapse from here. Do it now, or the choice is made for us.
Valerius raises his hand, his eyes clouded with the familiar weight of sacrifice. He unleashes a concentrated wave of energy toward the stone archway.
The archway buckles, bringing down tons of rock. The screams of the vanguard—and the trapped traditionalists—are suddenly cut short, replaced by a massive cloud of dust.

Lyra watches the collapse, her knuckles white around her hilt. She looks at Kaelen, her eyes burning with pure, unadulterated resentment, yet she raises her sword to signal the surviving archers toward the creek bed.
I will never forgive you for this.
I did not ask for your forgiveness. Move your archers.
elements.expression: indifferent, already looking toward the next ridge
